purpose: Rolling coverage memory for the adaptive daily ingest. Used to avoid repeated sources/themes and to rotate toward coverage gaps.

runs.editorial_thesis: The actionable shift is from isolated AI features to governed systems that connect work context, design delivery and execution without dropping review boundaries.
Put security in the review loop, make autonomous delivery explicit and inspectable, and audit what delegation is actually buying.

selected_lanes.title: Delivery Observability + Portfolio Hygiene
selected_lanes.selection_rationale: A new GitHub Projects capability, deployment/cache diagnostics, and macOS toolchain preview form a practical, under-covered delivery lane. These are fresh primary releases, not a repeat of yesterday’s agent-governance discussion.

selected_lanes.title: Hybrid Agent Workflows
selected_lanes.selection_rationale: This lane follows a material design shift: deterministic code owns mandatory transitions while models own ambiguity. It builds on, rather than repeats, yesterday’s reliable-delivery coverage with a newly tracked framework, production case study, and fresh robustness evidence.

selected_lanes.title: Creative Client Communications
selected_lanes.selection_rationale: Creative production and mobile field workflows have not been a recent lane. Fresh Google releases show a fast route from a rough prompt or screen recording to a reviewable client-facing update, with provenance and eligibility limits worth keeping visible.

last_run.editorial_thesis: The useful signal today is not more AI surface area: it is inspectable delivery, deterministic boundaries around agents, and fast creative drafts with human approval.
